settings.title: Settings

general.title: General
general.restoreWorkspaceDesc: Automatically open the last active workspace when the app starts
general.launchAtLoginDesc: Start Octopal automatically when you log in
general.autoVersionHistoryDesc: Automatically save a snapshot after each agent action, so you can track and undo changes

language.title: Language
language.desc: Choose the display language for the app

agents.title: Default Agent Permissions
agents.desc: New agents will start with these permissions. You can override per-agent in the agent settings.
agents.fileWriteDesc: Allow agents to create and edit files
agents.shellDesc: Allow agents to execute bash/shell commands
agents.networkDesc: Allow agents to make web requests

appearance.title: Appearance
appearance.interfaceFontDesc: Used for sidebars, buttons, menus, and other UI chrome.
appearance.chatFontDesc: Used for message content and markdown body text.
appearance.codeFontDesc: Used for inline code and fenced code blocks.
appearance.fontPreview: The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog.

shortcuts.title: Shortcuts
shortcuts.textExpansionsDesc: Define shortcuts that expand when you send a message. Type the trigger (e.g. /fix) and it auto-expands. Saves tokens and speeds up your workflow.
shortcuts.noShortcuts: No shortcuts yet. Add one to speed up your workflow!
shortcuts.triggerMinLength: Trigger must be at least 2 characters
shortcuts.suggestionsDesc: Quick-add common shortcuts based on your agents:

advanced.title: Advanced
advanced.autoModelSelectionDesc: Automatically choose the optimal AI model based on task complexity. When off, all agents use the default model.
advanced.defaultAgentModelDesc: Fixed model for all agent responses when adaptive selection is disabled.
advanced.opusDetected: Detected on your machine: {{model}} — Octopal will use it automatically when Opus is selected.
advanced.backupDesc: Octopal snapshots files before agents modify them so you can undo. Snapshots live in each project's .octopal/backups folder.
advanced.backupMaxCountDesc: Older snapshots are sent to the OS trash when this limit is exceeded.
advanced.backupMaxAgeDesc: Snapshots older than this are pruned regardless of count.
advanced.runtimeDesc: Which backend spawns your agents. Defaults to the legacy Claude CLI while Goose 2.0 is in beta.
advanced.useLegacyCliDesc: When on, agents spawn via the Claude CLI (requires `claude` installed globally). Turn off to use the bundled Goose 2.0 sidecar. Configure API keys in Settings → Providers.

models.runtimeDesc: Which backend spawns your agents. Other settings on this page only take effect for the selected runtime — Octopal will keep both choices saved so toggling preserves them.
models.runtimeClaudeCliDesc: Spawns agents through your installed `claude` CLI. Uses Claude's own auth — API keys below are not consumed in this mode.
models.runtimeGooseDesc: Bundled Goose 2.0 sidecar. Reads provider/model and API keys configured below.
models.runtimeApplyHint: Runtime change applies after Save, starting from the next message.
models.keysClaudeCliBanner: Claude CLI runtime uses `claude`'s own auth. These keys are only consumed by the Goose runtime — you can still save them in advance.

providers.title: Providers
providers.desc: Configure the AI providers your agents use. Keys are stored in your OS keyring (macOS Keychain / Windows Credential Manager / Linux Secret Service).
providers.defaultProviderDesc: Used by agents that don't specify a provider of their own.
providers.defaultModelDesc: Anthropic aliases (opus/sonnet/haiku) are resolved to the latest Octopal-curated model at run time.
providers.plannerModelDesc: Model used by the dispatcher to pick the responding agent. Schema-only in v0.2.0-beta — the dispatcher still uses Haiku hardcoded until Stage 6b-ii ships; your choice here is saved and will take effect then.
providers.confirmRemove: Remove the stored key for {{name}}? Your OS keyring entry will be deleted.
providers.statusKeyring: OS keyring connected. Keys never leave this machine.
providers.statusEnvFallback: Running in environment-variable fallback mode. Keys are read from OCTOPAL_KEY_<PROVIDER> env vars, bypassing OS keyring. Not recommended on shared machines. ({{envVar}}=env)
providers.statusUnavailable: OS keyring unavailable. Install a Secret Service daemon, or set OCTOPAL_API_KEY_FALLBACK=env (see docs/troubleshooting-linux-keyring.md).
providers.fallbackHint: Set {{envVar}} in the environment that launches Octopal.
providers.manifestUnavailable: Provider manifest couldn't be loaded. Restart the app or check logs.

settings.saveFailedMessage: Settings could not be written to disk. Please try again.

chat.placeholder: Message the room… (@name to target one agent, @all for everyone)
chat.placeholderDisabled: Open a folder to start chatting
chat.emptyWithFolder: Use @name to talk to an agent. @all to talk to everyone.
chat.emptyNoFolder: Open a folder to get started.
chat.routing: Routing message to the right agent…

conversations.deleteConfirm: Delete conversation "{{title}}"? This cannot be undone.
conversations.createFailedMessage: Saving the new conversation failed. Please try again.
conversations.switchFailedMessage: Loading the conversation history failed. Please try again.
conversations.renameFailedMessage: Saving the new title failed. Please try again.
conversations.deleteFailedMessage: Removing the conversation failed. Please try again.
conversations.persistFailedMessage: Your message is shown locally but could not be written to disk. It may be lost on reload.

agents.title: Agents
agents.clickToMention: Click to mention, right-click to edit
agents.limitMsg:
You can create up to {{max}} agents per folder.
Delete an existing agent and try again.

welcome.title: Welcome to Octopal!
welcome.desc:
Start by opening a project folder
to chat with your AI teammates.

createAgent.title: New agent
createAgent.permissionsHint: Without any of these, the agent can only reply with text. Turn one on to let it act.

editAgent.title: Edit agent
editAgent.deleteConfirm: Delete {{name}}? This removes the agent folder permanently.
editAgent.rolePlaceholder: Full-stack developer specializing in React and TypeScript
editAgent.roleHint: Short description shown to other agents (1-2 sentences).
editAgent.promptHint: Full instructions for this agent. Saved as prompt.md — also editable in VS Code or any text editor.
editAgent.promptPlaceholder: You are a developer agent. You write clean code, implement features, fix bugs...

createWorkspace.title: New workspace
createWorkspace.hint: Start by creating a workspace. You can have multiple workspaces for different contexts — work, side projects, experiments.

openFolder.desc: Open a project folder to get started.

claudeLogin.descNotInstalled: Octopal requires the Claude CLI to communicate with agents. Please install it first:
claudeLogin.descNotLoggedIn: You need to log in to Claude CLI before using Octopal. Open a terminal and run:
claudeLogin.hint: After logging in, restart Octopal or dismiss this dialog.
claudeLogin.retryFailed: Claude CLI still not found. Please install and log in, then try again.

activity.title: Recent Activity
activity.revertRunTitle: Revert all changes from this run

timeline.title: Change History
timeline.emptyDesc: Changes will appear here after agents complete their work
timeline.revertConfirm: This will undo {{count}} change(s) after this point. Continue?
timeline.autoPushDesc: Automatically push to remote after changes
timeline.rollbackNotice: Previous work undone, restarting with new instructions
timeline.nearCompletion: Almost done, will deliver additional instructions after completion

wiki.title: Wiki
wiki.emptyDesc:
Shared notes the whole team can read and write.
Create a page to get started.

app.windowLimit: You can open up to {{max}} windows.
app.removeWorkspaceConfirm: Remove this workspace? Folders inside remain on disk.
app.waiting: Waiting — {{name}} is still working on a previous message…
app.bundleModify: ⏸️ @{{agent}} Stopping current task to deliver updated instructions.
app.bundleMultiStop: ⏸️ Stopping {{agents}} ({{count}} agents) to deliver new instructions.
app.interruptConfirm: ⚠️ {{count}} agents are currently working ({{agents}}). Interrupting will discard all in-progress work. Proceed?
app.chainComplete: ✅ Team task complete — {{agents}} ({{count}} agents) participated, took {{elapsed}}s
app.permRetryPrompt:
(Previously couldn't perform task due to lack of permissions. User approved permissions. Please continue with the original request.)

User's original request: {{request}}

mcp.title: MCP Servers
mcp.hint: Connect external tools (Figma, GitHub, Slack, etc.) via Model Context Protocol. Paste a JSON config or leave empty.
mcp.jsonError: Invalid JSON. Please check the format.

global.title: Global MCP Servers
global.desc: Servers defined here are available to every agent unless an agent disables them.
global.confirmDelete: Delete "{{name}}" from the global registry? Agents that don't override this server will lose access.

edit.name: Name
edit.nameHint: Lowercase, no spaces — used as the server identifier.

errors.nameInvalid: Name must be lowercase letters, numbers, hyphens
errors.commandRequired: Command is required for stdio servers
errors.urlRequired: URL is required for HTTP servers

agentMcp.title: MCP Servers for this agent
agentMcp.desc: Toggle which servers and tools this agent can use. Global servers are inherited.
agentMcp.toolsUnavailable: Could not list tools — check server is reachable.
agentMcp.noEffectiveServers: No MCP servers available. Add one in Settings → MCP.
agentMcp.globalLoadFailed: Could not load global MCP servers — only local agent servers are shown.

mcpValidation.title: MCP Server Validation
mcpValidation.authError: {{server}} token has expired or is invalid
mcpValidation.authErrorHint: Go to agent settings and enter a new token. (e.g., Figma > Settings > Personal Access Tokens)
mcpValidation.networkErrorHint: Check your internet connection and try again. A firewall or proxy may be blocking the connection.
mcpValidation.installFailedHint: Try installing manually in the terminal, or check your network connection.
mcpValidation.serverConnected: {{server}} MCP server is connected and ready!
mcpValidation.allConnected: All MCP servers are connected and ready!
mcpValidation.hasIssues: The server has issues. The agent will still work, but without MCP tools.
mcpValidation.tokenExpiryMessage: Agent "{{agent}}"'s {{server}} token has expired or is invalid. Please enter a new token in settings.

security.accessRequestDesc: Agent "{{agent}}" is trying to access a file outside the project.
security.blockedDesc: This path is restricted by the security policy.
